text: The video begins with a view of the left chest anatomy, highlighting the aortic arch. The pulmonary vessels are visible, including both the inferior and superior pulmonary veins, with the superior pulmonary vein clearly identified.
The surgeon identifies the inferior pulmonary vein below the pulmonary ligament. The pulmonary ligament is being divided to expose the inferior pulmonary vein during dissection in the hilum.

The surgeon has divided the fissure, revealing the branches of the pulmonary artery that supply the upper lobe and lingula. As the lung is lifted, the pericardium and the phrenic nerve become visible.
The video shows the internal mammary vessels and the sympathetic chain near the thoracic inlet, with the subclavian vessel visible in the surgical field.
